headline: Fighter Jets 💥🛩️ COLLIDE MID-AIR 🌬️ - Crews PARACHUTE OUT 🍑🪂 SAFE AF 😎
text: Fighter jet crews 🛩️🍑 PARACHUTED SAFELY 🪂😇 after mid-air COLLISION 💥🤯 at US air show 🎪🔥! Four crew members 🧑‍✈️👨‍✈️ are in STABLE CONDITION 🏥💪 after two EA-18G Growler jets 🛩️⚡ crashed mid-air during a DEMONSTRATION 💥🎭 at the Gunfighter Skies air show in Idaho 🗻🏠! All aircrew SAFELY EJECTED 🪂🚀 from the jets that were PERFORMING AERIAL STUNTS 🕺🌬️ when they SLAMMED 💥🤕 into each other like two DRUNK PILOTS 🍺✈️ at a tailgate party! The dramatic incident happened SUNDAY 🗓️☀️ during the second and final day of the show, 2 miles northwest of Mountain Home Air Force Base 🏠🛡️, where a FIRE ERUPTED 🔥😱 and the base was BRIEFLY LOCKED DOWN 🔒🚨 as the rest of the air show got CANCELLED 🛑😭! An investigation is UNDERWAY 🕵️‍♂️🔍 like a detective drama on Netflix 📺🍿! 'The aircrew are in STABLE CONDITION' 🏥💪 said Mountain Air Force Base Gunfighters in a statement on social media 📱🔥, thanking all guests for their PATIENCE and COMPASSION 🙏😇 which allowed them to QUICKLY and SAFELY respond to the incident! The Navy spokesperson said the crew members were being CHECKED by medical personnel 🩺👩‍⚕️, and each of those $67M jets 💰🤑 was assigned to an electronic attack squadron from Washington state like they were on a TOP GUN MISSION 🎬✈️! Kim Sykes of Silver Wings of Idaho said no one at the base was HURT 🤕😌, and the air show was last held in 2018 when a glider pilot DIED in an accident 🪦😢, but this time EVERYONE GOT OUT ALIVE 🪂🎉 like a real-life action movie with no casualties except maybe some bruised egos 😂💥! The BBC contacted the base and event organizer for comment 📞📰, but the crews are already back to SLAYING the skies another day 🛩️💪!
